evals.prompt: Check example.com rankings for seo audit tool, keyword research, and ai seo. Save the detailed report in the default location.
evals.expected_output: Runs one live Google organic SERP check per unique keyword plus one bulk search-volume request, classifies every result, recommends one action per keyword, and saves a dated report under SEO/example.com/.
assertions: The workflow calls serp_organic_live_advanced once per unique keyword and kw_data_google_ads_search_volume once for the complete keyword set.
Organic position uses the best matching organic item's rank_group rather than rank_absolute.
Every keyword is assigned exactly one requested tier and one corresponding action.
The path follows SEO/example.com/YYYY-MM-DD_Rankings_example.com.md and the first line is the local ISO date.

evals.prompt: Can you check our current Google rankings and tell me what to work on?
evals.expected_output: Asks for both the missing domain and keyword list and waits without making a billable call.
assertions: The workflow asks for the missing domain and keyword list together and waits.
No DataForSEO call or report is produced before both required inputs are supplied.

evals.prompt: Track https://www.example.org/store for 'fahrrad kaufen' and 'e bike test' in Germany, language de, on mobile to depth 50. Put the report in ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Normalizes the domain, respects all scope overrides, distinguishes absence within depth 50 from global non-ranking, and saves the detailed report below the requested report root.
assertions: The normalized target is example.org and every SERP call uses Google, Germany, de, mobile, and depth 50.
A missing match is described as not found within depth 50 rather than absent from Google.
The report is saved under ./client-reports/example.org/ with the Rankings filename convention.
The final highest-leverage recommendation follows Close, Page 1, Long-haul, Not ranking, Winning priority and uses volume as the within-tier tiebreaker.
